questions.question: Which default condition must be considered when an encrypted mobility tunnel is used between two Cisco WLCs?

options.A: The tunnel uses the EoIP protocol to transmit data traffic.
options.B: TCP port 443 and UDP 21 are used.
options.C: The tunnel uses the IPsec protocol for encapsulation.
options.D: Control and data traffic encryption are enabled.

questions.explanation:
A secure link in which data is encrypted using CAPWAP DTLS protocol (-> Answer C is not correct) can be established between two controllers. This secured link is called Encrypted Mobility Tunnel.
If encrypted mobility tunnel is in enabled state, the data traffic is encrypted and the controller uses UDP port 16667 (-> Answer B is not correct), instead of EoIP (-> Answer A is not correct), to send the data traffic.
In Release 8.5.164.0, when encryption is enabled on a controller, by default both control and data traffic is encrypted (-> Answer D is correct). However, based on your network requirements you can disable or enable data traffic encryption on the controller.
Reference:

questions.question: Why does an administrator choose to implement a remote access IPsec VPN?

questions.explanation: Remote access IPsec VPNs are used to provide secure, encrypted connections for individual users to a corporate network over the internet. They enable remote employees to access internal resources, such as servers, databases, and applications, from any location, extending the reach of the private network. This is achieved by establishing a secure IPsec tunnel, which uses encryption to protect data and authentication to verify the identity of users and devices.

questions.question: Which two principles must be considered when using per-hop behavior in QoS? (Choose two)

options.A: Policing is not supported on subinterfaces.
options.B: Shaping and rate limiting have the same effect.
options.C: Shaping drops excessive traffic without adding traffic delay.
options.D: Shaping levels out traffic bursts by delaying excess traffic.
options.E: Policing is performed in the inbound and outbound directions.

questions.explanation:
+ Policing: is used to control the rate of traffic flowing across an interface. During a bandwidth exceed (crossed the maximum configured rate), the excess traffic is generally dropped or remarked. The result of traffic policing is an output rate that appears as a saw-tooth with crests and troughs. Traffic policing can be applied to inbound and outbound interfaces. Unlike traffic shaping, QoS policing avoids delays due to queuing. Policing is configured in bytes.
+ Shaping: retains excess packets in a queue and then schedules the excess for later transmission over increments of time. When traffic reaches the maximum configured rate, additional packets are queued instead of being dropped to proceed later. Traffic shaping is applicable only on outbound interfaces as buffering and queuing happens only on outbound interfaces. Shaping is configured in bits per second.
questions.question_vi: Hai nguyên tắc nào phải được xem xét khi sử dụng hành vi theo từng chặng (per-hop behavior) trong QoS? (Chọn hai)

options_vi.A: Policing không được hỗ trợ trên các subinterface.
options_vi.B: Shaping và rate limiting có cùng hiệu quả.
options_vi.C: Shaping loại bỏ lưu lượng vượt mức mà không thêm độ trễ lưu lượng.
options_vi.D: Shaping làm phẳng các đợt bùng phát lưu lượng bằng cách trì hoãn lưu lượng vượt mức.
options_vi.E: Policing được thực hiện theo cả hướng vào (inbound) và hướng ra (outbound).

questions.question: What differentiates the TCP and UDP protocols?

options.A: TCP tracks segments being transmitted or received by assigning segment numbers, and UDP adjusts data flow according to network conditions.
options.B: TCP establishes a connection with the device on the other end before transferring, and UDP transfers without establishing a connection.
options.C: TCP sends data at a constant rate with error checking on upper protocol layers, and UDP provides error- checking and sequencing.
options.D: TCP immediately transmits data without waiting for a handshake, and UDP awaits a response from the receiver before sending additional data.

questions.question_vi: Điều gì phân biệt giao thức TCP và UDP?

options_vi.A: TCP theo dõi các phân đoạn đang được truyền hoặc nhận bằng cách gán số phân đoạn, và UDP điều chỉnh luồng dữ liệu theo điều kiện mạng.
options_vi.B: TCP thiết lập kết nối với thiết bị ở đầu kia trước khi truyền, và UDP truyền mà không thiết lập kết nối.
options_vi.C: TCP gửi dữ liệu ở tốc độ không đổi với kiểm tra lỗi trên các lớp giao thức phía trên, và UDP cung cấp kiểm tra lỗi và sắp xếp thứ tự.
options_vi.D: TCP truyền dữ liệu ngay lập tức mà không chờ bắt tay (handshake), và UDP chờ phản hồi từ bên nhận trước khi gửi dữ liệu bổ sung.

For example, when you open your phone’s Wi-Fi list and see names like “Home_WiFi” or “CoffeeShop_Free_WiFi”;  those are SSIDs.

options_vi.B: Sử dụng gratuitous ARP để cập nhật bộ nhớ đệm ARP (ARP cache) của client khi router active chuyển đổi.
options_vi.C: Yêu cầu các tuyến đường active và standby để cung cấp chuyển đổi dự phòng (failover) trong trường hợp router bị lỗi.
options_vi.D: Sử dụng địa chỉ MAC ảo (virtual MAC addressing) để cung cấp khả năng dự phòng gateway trên mạng LAN.

questions.question: What is a characteristic of private IPv4 addressing?

options.A: reduces the forwarding table on network routers
options.B: traverses the internet when an outbound ACL is applied
options.C: address space which is isolated from the internet
options.D: eliminates the need for routing between devices

questions.explanation:
Private IPv4 addressing refers to IP addresses that are not routable on the public internet. These addresses are intended for use within internal networks; for example, in homes, offices, or enterprise LANs…
The Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) reserved specific ranges for private use:
+ 10.0.0.0 – 10.255.255.255 (10.0.0.0/8)+ 172.16.0.0 – 172.31.255.255 (172.16.0.0/12)+ 192.168.0.0 – 192.168.255.255 (192.168.0.0/16)
Because these addresses are isolated from the internet, they cannot be reached directly from public networks. To communicate externally, devices using private IPs typically rely on Network Address Translation (NAT), which converts private addresses to a public one for internet access.
questions.question_vi: Đặc điểm của địa chỉ IPv4 riêng (private IPv4 addressing) là gì?

options_vi.A: Giảm bảng chuyển tiếp (forwarding table) trên các router mạng
options_vi.B: Đi qua internet khi một ACL outbound được áp dụng
options_vi.C: Không gian địa chỉ được cách ly khỏi internet
options_vi.D: Loại bỏ nhu cầu định tuyến giữa các thiết bị

questions.question: An application in the network is being scaled up from 300 servers to 600. Each server requires 3 network connections to support production, backup, and management traffic. Each connection resides on a different subnet. The router configuration for the production network must be configured first using a subnet in the 10.0.0.0/8 network. Which command must be configured on the interface of the router to accommodate the requirements and limit wasted IP address space?
questions.explanation:
600 servers -> 600 connections for production (we don’t care about backup and management connections as “each connection resides on a different subnet”)-> 600 IP addresses in the same subnet. Therefore we need at least 210 = 1024 hosts/subnet(>600) -> We need 10 bit 0 in the subnet mask -> 11111111.11111111.1111 1100.0000 0000 -> 255.255.252.0 -> Answer D is correct.
Note: In fact, we need to subtract 2 from 210 for the host’s calculation but we ignored it because it does not affect the result.
questions.question_vi: Một ứng dụng trong mạng đang được mở rộng từ 300 máy chủ lên 600. Mỗi máy chủ yêu cầu 3 kết nối mạng để hỗ trợ lưu lượng production, backup và management. questions.question: Which statement describes virtual machines?

options.A: They include a guest OS and the service.
options.B: They facilitate local management of infrastructure devices.
options.C: They use a supervisor to provide management for services.
options.D: They enable the network to become agile and hardware-centric.

questions.explanation: A virtual machine is a software-based emulation of a physical computer. It runs an operating system (guest OS) and applications (services) on top of a hypervisor, which manages multiple VMs on a single physical host.
questions.question_vi: Phát biểu nào mô tả máy ảo (virtual machines)?

options_vi.A: Chúng bao gồm hệ điều hành khách (guest OS) và dịch vụ.
options_vi.B: Chúng hỗ trợ quản lý cục bộ các thiết bị hạ tầng.
options_vi.C: Chúng sử dụng một supervisor để cung cấp quản lý cho các dịch vụ.
options_vi.D: Chúng cho phép mạng trở nên linh hoạt và tập trung vào phần cứng.

questions.question: Which combination of methods satisfies the minimum security requirements when a new multifactor authentication solution is deployed?

options.A: password of 8 to 15 characters and personal 12-digit PIN
options.B: authorized USB dongle and mobile phone
options.C: complex password and time-based one-time password

questions.explanation:
Multifactor authentication (MFA) enhances security by requiring an additional authentication factor, such as:
+ Something you know (password or PIN…).+ Something you have (a hardware token or mobile app…).+ Something you are (biometric authentication like a fingerprint…).
Therefore the correct answer must have at least two different factors mentioned above.

Refer to the exhibit.
After a recent internal security audit, the network administrator decided to block all P2P-capable devices from the selected SSID. Which configuration setting must the administrator apply?

options.A: Set the P2P Block Action to Drop.
options.B: Select a correctly configured Layer 2 ACL.
options.C: Set the Wi-Fi Direct Client Policy to Not-Allow.
options.D: Set the MFP Client Protection to Required.

questions.explanation:
Features
Purpose
P2P Blocking Action
Controls client-to-client communication within the same SSID through the AP(intra-BSS traffic). But it does not stop Wi-Fi Direct functionality specifically. It only stops communication through the AP, not direct device-to-device links.
Wi-Fi Direct Client Policy
Controls Wi-Fi Direct-capable devices (P2P devices) – that is, clients that can form a direct connection (device-to-device) without going through the access point.
-> Answer C is the best choice as it does not allow P2P-capable devices connect to the AP from the beginning.

For APs in Flexconnect mode, when using locally switched WLANs mapped to different VLANs (AP switch port is on trunk mode), prune or limit the VLANs present on the port to match the AP configured VLANs.
Reference:
When an AP is running in FlexConnect mode, it can locally switch traffic at the remote site without tunneling all user data back to the WLC.

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
The loopback IP of R3 has been learned via the two interfaces on R1. R1 is configured with a reference bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Based on the metric calculations, which next hop IP would be used for outgoing routing?
questions.explanation:
OSPF cost is calculated using the formula: Cost = Reference Bandwidth [Mpbs] / Interface Bandwidth [Mpbs].
“R1 is configured with a reference bandwidth of 10 Gbps”. Therefore the cost on each interface is calculated as follows:
+ Upper link: Cost = 10 / 1 = 10+ Lower link: Cost = 10 / 10 = 1
-> The lower link has lower cost which is better -> R1 will choose the lower link for outgoing routing -> Answer D is correct.
Note: As both Reference Bandwidth & Interface Bandwidth use Gpbs, we don’t need to change from Gpbs to Mpbs in the calculation.
questions.question_vi:
Tham khảo hình minh họa.
Địa chỉ IP loopback của R3 đã được học qua hai interface trên R1. R1 được cấu hình với reference bandwidth là 10 Gbps. Dựa trên tính toán metric, địa chỉ IP next hop nào sẽ được sử dụng cho định tuyến đi ra?

questions.question: Which feature of Digest Authentication prevents credentials from being sent in plaintext?
questions.explanation:
Digest authentication is a secure method for web servers to verify a user’s identity by using a challenge-response system to avoid sending plain-text passwords.
Here’s how it works:
1. The server sends a nonce (a random value) as a challenge to the client.2. The client uses this nonce, along with the username, password, and other details, to generate a hash (typically MD5).3. The client sends this hash to the server (not the plaintext password).4. The server performs the same hash operation on its side and compares the results to authenticate the user.

options_vi.B: Cơ chế challenge-response

How will router R1 handle packets destined to 192.168.64.22?

options.B: It will use the route with the highest AD and highest destination IP.
options.C: It will route the packets to 10.1.1.2.

questions.explanation:
192.168.64.0/19 subnet has increment of 32 so it ranges from 192.168.64.0 to 192.168.64.31 which covers destination of 192.168.64.22. Therefore traffic destined to 192.168.64.22 matches the output line “S 192.168.64.0/19 [1/0] via Null0” so it will be forwarded to Null0 interface, which means it will be dropped.
Note: A Null0 interface is a virtual “black hole” interface in a router that discards any traffic sent to it, and it’s primarily used to prevent routing loops, often when route summarization is configured. We can create above Null0 entry in the routing table with command “ip route 19.268.64.0 255.255.224.0 null0”.

options_vi.A: Nó sẽ sử dụng tuyến tĩnh (static route) đến 10.1.1.1.
options_vi.B: Nó sẽ sử dụng tuyến có AD cao nhất và IP đích cao nhất.
options_vi.C: Nó sẽ định tuyến các gói tin đến 10.1.1.2.

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
HQC needs to use a configuration that:
* Handles up to 150,000 concurrent connections* Minimizes consumption of public IP addresses
Which configuration meets the requirements?

options.A: ip nat pool NATPOOL 209.165.201.1 209.165.201.5 netmask 255.255.255.248ip nat inside source list HQC interface gigabitEthernet0/0 overload
options.B: ip nat pool NATPOOL 209.165.200.225 209.165.200.226 netmask 255.255.255.252ip nat outside source list HQC pool NATPOOL overload
options.C: ip nat pool NATPOOL 209.165.201.1 209.165.201.3 netmask 255.255.255.248ip nat inside source list HQC pool NATPOOL overload
options.D: ip nat pool NATPOOL 209.165.201.1 209.165.201.248 netmask 255.255.255.248ip nat outside source list HQC pool NATPOOL overload

questions.explanation:
A Cisco router using PAT can theoretically handle up to 64,511 ports per public IP address. These ports are generally in the 1024 to 65535 range to avoid conflicts with well-known ports (0-1023).
Therefore in order to “handles up to 150,000 concurrent connections”, we need 150,000/64,511 = 2.3. Also to “minimizes consumption of public IP addresses” -> We need a minimum of 3 public IP addresses -> The range “209.165.201.1 209.165.201.3” is the best choice.

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
A network engineer must configure the CPE router to allow computers in the 172.20.1.0/24 network to obtain their IP configurations from the central DHCP server. Which configuration must the engineer apply to the CPE?
questions.explanation:
If the DHCP Server is not on the same subnet with the DHCP Client, we need to configure the router on the DHCP client side to act as a DHCP Relay Agent so that it can forward DHCP messages between the DHCP Client & DHCP Server. To make a router a DHCP Relay Agent, simply put the “ip helper-address <IP-address-of-DHCP-Server>” command under the interface that receives the DHCP messages from the DHCP Client.
As we know, router does not forward broadcast packets (it drops them instead) so DHCP messages like DHCPDISCOVER message will be dropped. But with the “ip helper-address …” command, the router will accept that broadcast message and cover it into a unicast packet and forward it to the DHCP Server. The destination IP address of the unicast packet is taken from the “ip helper-address …” command.

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
An engineer was asked to update a wireless LAN controller configuration on a newly deployed SSID “Office”. However, the configuration was not well documented or commented. What can the engineer determine about this configuration?

options.A: There is an additional protection level that helps secure the data frames exchanged between wireless clients and the access points for all wireless devices.
options.B: There is an extended delay that helps minimizing the time it takes for client devices to stay connected after roaming activity for Apple and Android devices.
options.C: There is an advanced secure algorithm put into the service to add an extra level of quality assurance for wireless delivery networks.
options.D: There is a seamless transition mechanism used to expedite roaming for compatible devices by authenticating them before potential roaming occurs.

questions.explanation:
Protected Management Frame (PMF) protects unicast and broadcast management frames and encrypts unicast management frames. This means wireless intrusion detection and wireless intrusion prevention systems now have fewer brute-force ways to enforce client policies. PMF provides an additional layer of protection from de-authentication and disassociation attacks -> In the exhibit we see the “PMF” box was set to “Required” -> Answer A is not correct as PMF only protects management frames, not data frames.
Fast Transition (FT) is a new concept of roaming where the initial handshake with the new AP is done even before the client roams to the target AP. The initial handshake allows the client and APs to do the Pairwise Transient Key (PTK) calculation in advance. These PTK keys are applied to the client and AP after the client does the reassociation request or response exchange with new target AP -> Answer D is the best choice.
Note:
+ The comeback timer specifies the time that an associated client must wait before the association can be tried again when first denied with a status code 30.+ SA query timeout specifies the amount of time the WLC waits for a response from the client for the query process. If there is no response from the client, its association is deleted from the controller.

questions.question: What is the difference between the TCP and UDP protocols?

options.A: TCP ensures ordered, reliable data delivery, and UDP offers low latency and high throughput.
options.B: TCP manages multicast and broadcast data transfers, and UDP only handles unicast communications.
options.C: TCP discovers neighboring devices on a local network segment, and UDP prevents Layer 2 switching loops.
options.D: TCP identities devices by their MAC addresses, and UDP identities devices by their IP addresses.

questions.explanation: In general, UDP offers higher potential throughput than TCP because its lack of overhead, flow control, and retransmission mechanisms allows it to “flood” available bandwidth, especially in the presence of latency or packet loss -> Answer A is correct.

options_vi.B: TCP quản lý truyền dữ liệu multicast và broadcast, còn UDP chỉ xử lý truyền thông unicast.
options_vi.C: TCP phát hiện các thiết bị lân cận trên phân đoạn mạng cục bộ, còn UDP ngăn chặn vòng lặp chuyển mạch Layer 2.
options_vi.D: TCP nhận dạng thiết bị bằng địa chỉ MAC, còn UDP nhận dạng thiết bị bằng địa chỉ IP.

questions.question: Which type of VPN connection is used when an employee accesses a secure server from a public Wi-Fi?
questions.explanation:
Remote-access VPN is used when an individual user (such as an employee) connects securely to a private network or server over an untrusted network like public Wi-Fi.
Remote access VPNs are often used by Internet Protocol security (IPSec) teams. Over less secure networks, such as public Wi-Fi, a VPN establishes a secure and encrypted connection. A VPN application both encrypts and decrypts the data you send and receive. It also encrypts the network IP addresses from which data is sent and received.

questions.question: What is the role of nonoverlapping channels in a wireless environment?
questions.explanation:
The goal of using different non-overlapping channels is to avoid the affects caused by channel utilization and interference.
The 2.4 GHz band is subdivided into multiple channels each allotted 22 MHz bandwidth and separated from the next channel by 5 MHz.-> A best practice for 802.11b/g/n WLANs requiring multiple APs is to use non-overlapping channels such as 1, 6, and 11.
When two wireless devices transmit at the same time, their radio signals will collide and become garbled. 802.11 devices on the same channel use a CCA check to avoid these collisions. However, the CCA check may not detect a transmission occurring on a different channel that also has some frequency overlap on the channel the check is being performed on. In this case, two 802.11 devices on different channels that overlap may transmit at the same time causing a collision and possible data corruption or frame loss. This is called interference because one device’s transmission interferes with another device’s transmission.
Reference:

questions.question: What is the purpose of the logging facility?

options.A: It indicates the program or process that generated the syslog event.
options.B: It contains the text message that describes the syslog event.
options.C: It defines the severity of the syslog event.
options.D: It represents the timestamp of the syslog event.

questions.explanation:
A syslog message has the following format:
seq no:timestamp%FACILTY-SEVERITY-MNEMONIC: message text
Example: Jul 10 14:32:18.456: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/1, changed state to down
Each portion of a syslog message has a specific meaning. In which the FACILITY tells the protocol, module, or process that generated the message. Some examples are SYS for the operating system, IF for an interface…
All syslog messages have a logging facility and a level. The logging facility can be thought of as where and the level can be thought of as what.
Reference:
In the example above, the Facility: LINK  means interface/link-related events on Cisco devices.
questions.question_vi: Mục đích của trường facility trong logging là gì?

options_vi.A: Nó chỉ ra chương trình hoặc tiến trình đã tạo ra sự kiện syslog.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a CNAME record?

options.A: to associate an alias to a canonical domain name
options.B: to map a domain name to an IP address
options.C: to identify the authoritative name server for a domain
options.D: to direct email to a mail server

questions.explanation:
A CNAME (Canonical Name) record is used to create an alias that points to the true (canonical) domain name. This allows multiple domain names to refer to the same host. For example:
example.com (apex domain) -> has an A record pointing directly to your server’s IP address (e.g., 192.0.2.1).www.example.com (subdomain) -> has a CNAME record pointing to example.com.When someone visits www.example.com:1. DNS looks up the CNAME record and sees that www.example.com points to example.com.2. It checks the records for example.com.3. The A record for example.com provides the IP address of the server.4. The user is connected to the same destination as if they had typed example.com.

options_vi.C: Để xác định máy chủ tên có thẩm quyền (authoritative name server) cho một miền
options_vi.D: Để chuyển hướng email đến máy chủ thư (mail server)

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
ip route 172.0.0.0 255.0.0.0 10.12.13.14
ip route 172.16.0.0 255.255.0.0 10.56.22.23
ip route 122.16.153.0 255.255.255.0 10.35.47.17
ip route 172.16.153.153 255.255.255.255 10.65.34.19
ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 10.17.44.36
The static routes were implemented on the border router. What is the next hop IP address for a ping sent to 172.16.153.154 from the border router?
questions.explanation: The best match for the destination 172.16.153.154 is “ip route 172.16.0.0 255.255.0.0 10.56.22.23” so answer D is correct. If we don’t look each statement carefully, we may choose “ip route 122.16.153.0 255.255.255.0 …” by mistake (here is 122, not 172).

questions.question: Which cable type must be used when connecting a router and switch together?
questions.explanation:
To remember which type of cable you should use, follow these tips:
– To connect two serial interfaces of 2 routers we use serial cable– To specify when we use crossover cable or straight-through cable, we should remember:Group 1: Router, Host, ServerGroup 2: Hub, SwitchOne device in group 1 + One device in group 2: use straight-through cableTwo devices in the same group: use crossover cable
For example: we use straight-through cable to connect switch to router, switch to host, hub to host, hub to server… and we use crossover cable to connect switch to switch, switch to hub, router to router, host to host… )

options_vi.A: console
options_vi.B: rollover
